page: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a member of the Dlsv7 server family, built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by Intel processors. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 192 dedicated virtual CPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, positioning the hardware profile as a compute-focused, low-memory resource allocation.

The D192ls_v7 does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this server. The lack of integrated GPUs and local storage represents a clear tradeoff, optimizing the host resources purely for CPU-bound execution.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of 192 dedicated vCPUs relative to the 384.0 GB memory footprint makes this instance suitable for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory allocations. Appropriate workloads include large-scale batch processing, high-performance computing simulations, CPU-bound build systems, and distributed compute tasks where memory usage per thread is low.
description: The Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server within the Dlsv7 family, powered by Intel processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, establishing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With no integrated GPUs, the hardware profile is tailored for high-density CPU execution. This server is designed for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads that require massive processing power without a corresponding demand for large memory footprints, such as batch processing, CPU-bound simulations, and distributed compute tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is an Intel-powered server with 192 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. It features a compute-optimized 1:2 core-to-memory ratio and lacks local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D192ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 384.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring one hundred ninety-two dedicated cores and a lean two gigabytes of memory per vCPU.

384.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
Best suited for highly parallelized, CPU-bound workloads
